title: Experience
intro: 12+ years of experience building and leading production AI across drug safety, oncology, and clinical imaging — translating executive-sponsored research into measurable, multimillion-dollar business outcomes.
headline: Building AI at the frontier of medicine

roles.position: Senior Deep Learning Engineer / AI Associate Principal
roles.mission: Lead production AI across drug safety, oncology, and clinical imaging — translating executive-sponsored initiatives into measurable business outcomes.
roles.blurb: Leads production AI across drug-safety, oncology, and clinical-imaging programs.

bullets.text: AI Lead within a $3M+ SVP-sponsored drug safety initiative — building predictive toxicology models projected to save $20M-$50M annually by reducing animal studies, compressing safety timelines from 5 to 3 years, and improving compound prioritization.
Architected a model-agnostic multimodal AI platform integrating omics, imaging, and molecular features for drug safety prediction at scale.
Designed the platform for cross-assay, cross-compound scalability, integrating multi-vendor biological data across heterogeneous modalities.
Productionized ML pipelines with online learning, confidence estimation, multimodal augmentation, and joint embedding for drug safety prediction.
Oversaw 3 parallel drug safety delivery streams with a 12-member team, managing stakeholders, resources, risks, and technical direction.
Initiated a collaboration with Dr. Shantanu Singh (Senior Group Leader, Broad Institute) to co-develop computational pipelines for Cell Painting and omics.

projects.name: Tumor Recurrence Prediction — Translational Oncology

bullets.text: AI Lead for a Chief Scientist-sponsored oncology initiative developing a novel tumor recurrence prediction pipeline using single-cell transcriptomics.
Engineered a pipeline generalizable across drugs, treatment conditions, and heterogeneous data sources (organoids, xenografts, and clinical studies).
Integrated Med-PaLM LLM embeddings and AZ Biological Insights Knowledge Graph representations to enrich model inputs and improve signal quality.
Achieved 90% F1 on internal benchmarks across Osimertinib-treated cell lines and improved 13%+ over state-of-the-art baselines on public datasets.
Reduced pipeline compute time by 30% via Optuna-driven hyperparameter search and systematic profiling across training and evaluation stages.
Led a 2-member engineering team over 6 months, driving technical direction, experimentation strategy, roadmap ownership, and end-to-end delivery.

projects.subtitle: AI Technical Lead · Translational Oncology · Chief Scientist

bullets.text: Architected a reusable cross-platform framework to accelerate onboarding of new imaging use cases and enable rapid experimentation and deployment.
Designed the framework to standardize training, inference, and evaluation of vision foundation models, and deployed it across 3 internal infrastructures.
Scaled the foundation model framework across 3 clinical imaging modalities — CT, Endoscopy, and Echocardiography — and 3 internal AZ infrastructures.
Deployed the endoscopy vision model and inference pipeline from the framework in a clinical study for ranking gastrointestinal tissue damage severity.

roles.mission: Deliver high-impact oncology imaging AI with benchmark-beating performance that secured follow-on funding.
roles.blurb: Delivered a CT tumor-segmentation pipeline that beat state-of-the-art and secured follow-on funding.

bullets.text: Delivered a next-generation CT tumor segmentation pipeline in 6 months, with Dice scores 46% above SOTA, directly enabling $0.5M in follow-on funding.
Developed a radiologist-inspired dynamic window function and test-time augmentation, improving segmentation Dice by 11% and 7% respectively.
Exceeded stakeholder performance targets by 4-5x, achieving a 46% Dice improvement over state-of-the-art on NSCLC tumor segmentation benchmarks.
Validated pipeline generalizability across independent clinical trial datasets and challenging out-of-distribution imaging cohorts, including POSEIDON.
Reduced end-to-end training time by 20% and GPU memory by 15-20% through model parallelism and distributed data-parallel training strategies.

roles.mission: Build scalable foundation model platforms for digital pathology and computational imaging on AWS.
roles.blurb: Built AWS foundation-model and graph-neural-network platforms for digital pathology and clinical imaging.

bullets.text: AI Lead for an Executive Director-sponsored initiative developing a scalable, reusable histopathology foundation model platform on AWS infrastructure.
Architected an end-to-end AWS ML pipeline spanning data ingestion, preprocessing, distributed training, model interpretability, and inference.
Trained contrastive learning-based histopathology foundation models matching state-of-the-art benchmark performance at reduced GPU compute cost.
Engineered an ink-removal pipeline using classical image processing techniques, achieving performance parity with deep learning-based methods.
Built, deployed, and maintained a Label Studio annotation tool for pathologists within a GxP-compliant, multi-user internal AI-Bench environment.
Led a 4-member engineering team to deliver 2 contrastive and inpainting histopathology foundation model variants at 60% lower GPU compute costs.
Developed a Graph Neural Network-based pipeline for survival and response prediction on multiplex immunofluorescence histopathology slides.
Deployed graph-embedding pipelines to map the tumor microenvironment across NSCLC and HNSCC solid-tumor cohorts for response prediction.
Modularized preprocessing pipelines to extract and encode graphs with features derived from multispectral gigapixel histopathology images.

roles.mission: Ship production computer vision systems — face, video, and pose analytics — across enterprise deployments.
roles.blurb: Shipped production computer-vision systems for face, video, and pose analytics across enterprise deployments.

bullets.text: Delivered an enterprise face analytics platform with real-time recognition, verification, and identity clustering across two client deployments.
Automated model optimization, compression, and deployment pipelines specifically targeting resource-constrained edge production environments.
Accelerated enterprise use-case onboarding through modular, Docker-based components, configurable model pipelines, and scalable deployment tooling.
Engineered scalable video analytics pipelines for person re-identification (ReID) and multi-object tracking (MOT) across indoor and outdoor surveillance.
Developed robust label-based tracking algorithms resilient to lighting variability, camera motion, and multi-scene operational conditions.
Shipped a production-grade multi-environment pose estimation pipeline for an enterprise client spanning indoor, outdoor, and controlled settings.
Automated the activity recognition, behavioral monitoring, and event detection algorithms using TensorFlow-based pose-derived spatiotemporal features.
Engineered thermal-imaging-based physiological monitoring pipelines for real-time body vitals estimation across diverse operational environments.

roles.mission: Develop real-time clinical imaging systems — endoscopy, retinal imaging, and 3D neuroanatomy — for academic and clinical partners.
roles.blurb: Developed real-time clinical imaging systems spanning endoscopy, retinal imaging, and 3D neuroanatomy.

bullets.text: Shipped an end-to-end retinal image processing pipeline for clinical deployment in a first-of-its-kind 4K neonatal retinal imaging scanner.
Proposed and developed a digital Narrow Band Imaging (NBI) pipeline, eliminating dedicated hardware and reducing customer costs by ~$5K per device.
Led development and deployment of a clinical-grade real-time endoscopy video processing system with 10+ integrated modules on NVIDIA Jetson TX1.
Engineered real-time algorithms for polyp detection, blood vessel segmentation, illumination correction, enhancement, inpainting, and artifact removal.
Developed algorithms for cellular segmentation, cross-modality image alignment, and 3D reconstruction from multimodal serial-section 2D images.
Pioneered algorithms for neuron connectivity analysis, neuron tracing, and neuronal network topology mapping from coordinate-based 3D points.
Built and delivered a neuron-connectivity toolbox to Dr. Partha Mitra at Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory and researchers at RIKEN Brain Science Institute.
Championed Verification & Validation (V&V) protocols for an ultrasound-guided (US-guided) robotic navigation system for liver tumor ablation.
Designed and validated US-US and US-CT registration pipelines capable of aligning multimodal images with temporal and orientation variations.
Built 3D ultrasound reconstruction and oblique plane visualization workflows, and defined protocols for image acquisition, segmentation, and registration.

roles.mission: Bridge clinical, engineering, and commercial teams across 50+ hospital sites — driving adoption of medical imaging systems.
roles.blurb: Bridged clinical, engineering, and commercial teams across 50+ hospital sites to drive medical-imaging adoption.

bullets.text: Directed alignment as primary liaison across clinicians, biomedical engineers, sales teams, service teams, and hospital leadership spanning 50+ sites.
Built KOL networks and reference ecosystems through site visits, clinical demonstrations, and advocacy programs influencing purchasing decisions.
Headed value-based pre-sales engagements covering needs assessments, clinical evaluations, economic justification, and competitive displacement.
Developed commercial assets including competitive matrices, objection-handling guides, and case studies to strengthen market positioning.
Managed customer implementations including site setup, deployment, go-live execution, post-deployment stabilization, and proactive issue resolution.
Standardized clinical workflows with SOPs, safety protocols, checklists, quick-reference cards, and parameter preset libraries across sites.
Designed scalable train-the-trainer and role-based training programs across surgical specialties to accelerate adoption and sustained product utilization.
